name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart under a loaded barbell resting on your upper traps.
Unrack the bar and step back, keeping your chest up and core braced.
Lower your body by bending at the knees and hips until thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to return to the starting position.
tips: Keep your knees tracking over your toes throughout the movement.
Maintain a neutral spine — avoid rounding the lower back.
Brace your core before descending to protect the spine.

name: Pull-Up
instructions: Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, palms facing away.
Hang at full arm extension with shoulders engaged.
Pull your chest toward the bar by driving elbows down and back.
Lower yourself with control back to the starting position.
tips: Avoid swinging or kipping for strict strength gains.
Squeeze the bar hard to engage more upper-body muscles.
Cross your ankles and keep your legs still for better core engagement.

name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs.
Hinge at the hips, pushing them back while lowering the dumbbells along your legs.
Lower until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, keeping your back flat.
Drive your hips forward to return to the starting position.
tips: Keep the dumbbells close to your body throughout the movement.
Do not round the lower back — maintain a neutral spine.
Control the descent for maximum hamstring activation.

name: Push-Up
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands just outside shoulder-width.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows at roughly 45 degrees.
Stop just before your chest touches the ground.
Press back up to the starting position with arms fully extended.
tips: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels — no sagging hips.
Tuck your elbows slightly to protect your shoulder joints.
Breathe in on the way down, out on the way up.

name: Plank
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor with elbows directly below your shoulders.
Extend your legs behind you, resting on your toes.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Hold the position while breathing steadily.
tips: Squeeze your glutes and core to prevent your hips from rising or dropping.
Keep your neck neutral — don't look up or let your head hang.
Start with 20-second holds and progress gradually.
